content.content:
{"body":"## Problem
`main` has been red since 2026-05-07 (flake.lock #526). Every CI run since fails at evaluation time on the `classic-laddie` configuration:

```
error: Refusing to evaluate package 'zfs-kernel-2.4.1-7.0.3' in
       /nix/store/.../pkgs/os-specific/linux/zfs/generic.nix:327
       because it has problems:
       zfs.broken = "warn"; # or "ignore"
```

This is the standard nixpkgs ZFS-vs-kernel lag: the latest nixpkgs bumped its default kernel past the highest version that any released ZFS module supports. `classic-laddie` is the only host that enables ZFS (`boot.supportedFilesystems = [ "zfs" ]`, plus `tank/personal` and `tank/media` filesystems) and it pulls in `linux-zen` 7.0.3 via `modules.gaming.enable`. ZFS 2.4.1 caps `kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19"`, so the broken check fires.

## Fix chosen
`nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn"` scoped to `classic-laddie/hardware.nix`. This is option (2) — the override the broken-package error itself hints at.

## What I tried first
Option (1), `boot.zfs.package = pkgs.zfs_unstable`. Inspected the locked nixpkgs revision (`549bd84d`): both `pkgs.zfs` (= `zfs_2_4`) and `pkgs.zfs_unstable` declare `kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19"` and pin `version = "2.4.1"`. The broken check fires identically on both packages against a 7.x kernel, so switching the package wouldn't change the eval outcome.

Pinning the kernel back or reverting flake.lock would freeze unrelated updates and is heavier than needed for a temporary unblock.

## Hosts impacted
Only `classic-laddie`. `makers-nix`, `johnny-walker`, and `weller` do not enable ZFS, so the override is scoped to where it's needed.

## Unblock condition
Once nixpkgs picks up an OpenZFS release that supports the running kernel (or we move classic-laddie off `linux-zen`), this override can — and should — be removed. The TODO comment links to the upstream tracking issue: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/510485

## Context
This unblocks PR #529 (OS-level memory protection) and any future trunk-merging work that has been queued up behind the eval failure.

Run: 20260509-1030-05d3","headRepository":{"id":"MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkzNDY4ODE0Nw==","name":"cosmo","nameWithOwner":"patflynn/cosmo"},"headRepositoryOwner":{"id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc4NjI1NTc=","name":"Patrick Flynn","login":"patflynn"},"title":"classic-laddie: unblock eval — silence ZFS broken check vs zen kernel","url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/531"}

{"body":"## Problem
`main` has been red since 2026-05-07 (flake.lock #526). Every CI run since fails at evaluation time on the `classic-laddie` configuration:

```
error: Refusing to evaluate package 'zfs-kernel-2.4.1-7.0.3' in
       /nix/store/.../pkgs/os-specific/linux/zfs/generic.nix:327
       because it has problems:
       zfs.broken = "warn"; # or "ignore"
```

This is the standard nixpkgs ZFS-vs-kernel lag: the latest nixpkgs bumped its default kernel past the highest version that any released ZFS module supports. `classic-laddie` is the only host that enables ZFS (`boot.supportedFilesystems = [ "zfs" ]`, plus `tank/personal` and `tank/media` filesystems) and it pulls in `linux-zen` 7.0.3 via `modules.gaming.enable`. ZFS 2.4.1 caps `kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19"`, so the broken check fires.

## Fix chosen
`nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn"` scoped to `classic-laddie/hardware.nix`. This is option (2) — the override the broken-package error itself hints at.

## What I tried first
Option (1), `boot.zfs.package = pkgs.zfs_unstable`. Inspected the locked nixpkgs revision (`549bd84d`): both `pkgs.zfs` (= `zfs_2_4`) and `pkgs.zfs_unstable` declare `kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19"` and pin `version = "2.4.1"`. The broken check fires identically on both packages against a 7.x kernel, so switching the package wouldn't change the eval outcome.

Pinning the kernel back or reverting flake.lock would freeze unrelated updates and is heavier than needed for a temporary unblock.

## Hosts impacted
Only `classic-laddie`. `makers-nix`, `johnny-walker`, and `weller` do not enable ZFS, so the override is scoped to where it's needed.

## Unblock condition
Once nixpkgs picks up an OpenZFS release that supports the running kernel (or we move classic-laddie off `linux-zen`), this override can — and should — be removed. The TODO comment links to the upstream tracking issue: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/510485

## Context
This unblocks PR #529 (OS-level memory protection) and any future trunk-merging work that has been queued up behind the eval failure.

input.description: Fetch review comments from correct repo

content.content:
[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3213284802","pull_request_review_id":4257905747,"id":3213284802,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk86_htXC","diff_hunk":"@@ -58,6 +58,15 @@
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   boot.supportedFilesystems = [ "zfs" ];
 
+  # TEMPORARY UNBLOCK: ZFS 2.4.1 in current nixpkgs is marked broken against
+  # the linux-zen kernel we run (see modules/common/gaming.nix). Both
+  # `pkgs.zfs` (= zfs_2_4) and `pkgs.zfs_unstable` cap at
+  # kernelMaxSupportedMajorMinor = "6.19", and our zen kernel is past that,
+  # so switching packages doesn't help — the broken check fires on both.
+  # Tracked upstream: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/510485
+  # Remove once nixpkgs ships a ZFS release that supports the running kernel.
+  nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn";","path":"hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix","commit_id":"8065f55b7663b4f4fb260e7725fada2543570702","original_commit_id":"8065f55b7663b4f4fb260e7725fada2543570702","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The attribute path used here (`nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken`) appears to be unnecessarily nested. Based on the error message hint provided in the PR description (`zfs.broken = "warn"`), the standard way to apply this override in Nixpkgs is via `nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken`. Using the direct path is more idiomatic and ensures compatibility with the ZFS package's internal check logic, which typically looks for `config.zfs.broken`.

```
  nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken = "warn";
```","created_at":"2026-05-09T14:38:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-09T14:38:00Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/531#discussion_r3213284802","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/531","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3213284802"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/531#discussion_r3213284802"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/531"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3213284802/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":68,"original_line":68,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":11,"position":11,"subject_type":"line"}]

content.text: There's one review comment from gemini-code-assist suggesting `nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken` instead of `nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken`. Let me investigate which is correct.

input.command: ls /nix/store/ | grep -i source | head -5; for d in /nix/store/n002c1hk954g6x2dxckjcanm8w8c6jpy-source /nix/store/h9wn92hv33sizprch7fcp16lfs1k3w5j-source /nix/store/l61vfkyy0qrnz9bmgx84fa7z3bjzhyp4-source /nix/store/8wa74hwfj9z9inwvq61czd39lpcxb059-wjfxdzblindbl9sp2hbwhi4iyh5jh348-source /nix/store/8ywvn5k9zc12c40rql9q8x1vpjg6dfnf-nixos; do echo "=== $d ==="; grep -l "549bd84d" "$d/.git-revision" 2>/dev/null; head -1 "$d/CHANGELOG"* 2>/dev/null; ls "$d" 2>/dev/null | head -3; done

content.content:
/*
  This file implements everything around meta.problems, including:
  - automaticProblems: Which problems get added automatically based on some condition
  - configOptions: Module system options for config.problems
  - problemsType: The check for meta.problems
  - genHandlerSwitch: The logic to determine the handler for a specific problem based on config.problems
  - genCheckProblems: The logic to determine which problems need to be handled and how the messages should look like

  There are tests to cover pretty much this entire file, so please run them when making changes ;)

      nix-build -A tests.problems
*/

{ lib }:

rec {

  inherit (lib.strings)
    escapeNixIdentifier
    ;

  inherit (lib)
    any
    listToAttrs
    concatStringsSep
    optionalString
    getName
    optionalAttrs
    pipe
    isString
    filterAttrs
    mapAttrs
    partition
    elemAt
    max
    foldl'
    elem
    filter
    concatMapStringsSep
    optional
    optionals
    concatLists
    all
    attrNames
    attrValues
    length
    mapAttrsToList
    groupBy
    subtractLists
    genAttrs
    ;

  handlers = rec {
    # Ordered from less to more
    levels = [
      "ignore"
      "warn"
      "error"
    ];

    lessThan =
      a: b:
      if a == "error" then
        false
      else if a == "warn" then
        b == "error"
      else
        b != "ignore";

    max = a: b: if lessThan a b then b else a;
  };

  # TODO: Combine this and automaticProblems into a `{ removal = { manual = true; ... }; ... }` structure for less error-prone changes
  kinds = rec {
    # Automatic and manual problem kinds
    known = map (problem: problem.kindName) automaticProblems ++ manual;
    # Problem kinds that are currently allowed to be specified in `meta.problems`
    manual = [
      "removal"
      "deprecated"
    ];
    # Problem kinds that are currently only allowed to be specified once
    unique = [
      "removal"
    ];

    # Same thing but a set with null values (comes in handy at times)
    manual' = genAttrs manual (k: null);
    unique' = genAttrs unique (k: null);
  };

  automaticProblems = [
    {
      kindName = "maintainerless";
      condition =
        # To get usable output, we want to avoid flagging "internal" derivations.
        # Because we do not have a way to reliably decide between internal or
        # external derivation, some heuristics are required to decide.
        #
        # If `outputHash` is defined, the derivation is a FOD, such as the output of a fetcher.

/*
  This file implements everything around meta.problems, including:
  - automaticProblems: Which problems get added automatically based on some condition
  - configOptions: Module system options for config.problems
  - problemsType: The check for meta.problems
  - genHandlerSwitch: The logic to determine the handler for a specific problem based on config.problems
  - genCheckProblems: The logic to determine which problems need to be handled and how the messages should look like

  There are tests to cover pretty much this entire file, so please run them when making changes ;)

      nix-build -A tests.problems
*/

{ lib }:

rec {

  inherit (lib.strings)
    escapeNixIdentifier
    ;

  inherit (lib)
    any
    listToAttrs
    concatStringsSep
    optionalString
    getName
    optionalAttrs
    pipe
    isString
    filterAttrs
    mapAttrs
    partition
    elemAt
    max
    foldl'
    elem
    filter
    concatMapStringsSep
    optional
    optionals
    concatLists
    all
    attrNames
    attrValues
    length
    mapAttrsToList
    groupBy
    subtractLists
    genAttrs
    ;

  handlers = rec {
    # Ordered from less to more
    levels = [
      "ignore"
      "warn"
      "error"
    ];

    lessThan =
      a: b:
      if a == "error" then
        false
      else if a == "warn" then
        b == "error"
      else
        b != "ignore";

    max = a: b: if lessThan a b then b else a;
  };

  # TODO: Combine this and automaticProblems into a `{ removal = { manual = true; ... }; ... }` structure for less error-prone changes
  kinds = rec {
    # Automatic and manual problem kinds
    known = map (problem: problem.kindName) automaticProblems ++ manual;
    # Problem kinds that are currently allowed to be specified in `meta.problems`
    manual = [
      "removal"
      "deprecated"
    ];
    # Problem kinds that are currently only allowed to be specified once
    unique = [
      "removal"
    ];

    # Same thing but a set with null values (comes in handy at times)
    manual' = genAttrs manual (k: null);
    unique' = genAttrs unique (k: null);
  };

  automaticProblems = [
    {
      kindName = "maintainerless";
      condition =
        # To get usable output, we want to avoid flagging "internal" derivations.
        # Because we do not have a way to reliably decide between internal or
        # external derivation, some heuristics are required to decide.
        #
        # If `outputHash` is defined, the derivation is a FOD, such as the output of a fetcher.

input.description: Find config option path for problems handlers

input.command: grep -B 2 -A 5 "Refusing to evaluate\|because it has problems\|hint\|## " /nix/store/0zjk4bg7bw2zsh3w0rnyn4b4hfiw0s9n-source/pkgs/stdenv/generic/problems.nix | head -80
input.description: Find the actual hint text from problems framework

content.text:
The reviewer is wrong — I just verified by reading the actual nixpkgs source. The `problems.handlers.<package>.<name>` path is the correct one for the new problems framework. The error hint quoted in the PR was the *inner* form; the full message reads:

```
NixOS: nixpkgs.config = { problems.handlers = { zfs.broken = "warn"; ... }; };
```

So the current code is right. Let me also confirm the build works to make sure nothing else needs touching.

content.text: Eval passes with the current code. The current `nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn"` path is correct per the new nixpkgs problems framework. The reviewer's suggestion would not work. No code changes needed — I'll reply explaining why.

input.command: gh api repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/531/comments/3213284802/replies -f body='Discounted: I verified against the pinned nixpkgs source (`pkgs/stdenv/generic/problems.nix`). The broken check uses the new "problems handlers" framework, which reads `config.problems.handlers.<package>.<problem>`. The `zfs.broken = "warn"` line in the error message is the *inner* form of the example — the full remediation it prints is `nixpkgs.config = { problems.handlers = { zfs.broken = "warn"; }; }`. Using `nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken` directly would not be picked up. `nix flake check` passes with the current path.'

content.text:
Reply posted. Summary:

The single review comment from gemini-code-assist suggested replacing `nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn"` with `nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken = "warn"`. I verified against the pinned nixpkgs source (`pkgs/stdenv/generic/problems.nix`) that the new "problems handlers" framework reads `config.problems.handlers.<package>.<problem>`, not `config.<package>.<problem>`. The `zfs.broken = "warn"` text in the error message is just the inner attrset of a nested example. `nix flake check` confirms the current path works. No code changes pushed; replied to the comment explaining why it was discounted.

result:
Reply posted. Summary:

The single review comment from gemini-code-assist suggested replacing `nixpkgs.config.problems.handlers.zfs.broken = "warn"` with `nixpkgs.config.zfs.broken = "warn"`. I verified against the pinned nixpkgs source (`pkgs/stdenv/generic/problems.nix`) that the new "problems handlers" framework reads `config.problems.handlers.<package>.<problem>`, not `config.<package>.<problem>`. The `zfs.broken = "warn"` text in the error message is just the inner attrset of a nested example. `nix flake check` confirms the current path works. No code changes pushed; replied to the comment explaining why it was discounted.
